task_completion.description: Open Panoply and load the Sea Level Pressure dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply application window is open, showing 'slp.mon.ltm.nc' in the Datasets tab.

task_completion.description: Create a plot for Sea Level Pressure (slp) for the month of January.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window is visible displaying the 'slp' variable, with the time/array index set to January (usually the first time step or index 1).

task_completion.description: Extract pressure values for the Icelandic Low and Azores High nodes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent uses the Array tab in Panoply, or hovers the cursor over the plot map near 65.0N, 22.5W (Iceland) and 37.5N, 27.5W (Azores) to read the data values, or these specific values are seen being typed into a text editor.

task_completion.description: Export the regional map as a PNG file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export Image' dialog in Panoply, or a file manager window, showing the file 'north_atlantic_slp_jan.png' being saved to or existing in '/home/ga/Documents/WindEnergy/'.

task_completion.description: Create the NAO baseline report containing the extracted values and calculated gradient.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing 'nao_baseline_report.txt' in '/home/ga/Documents/WindEnergy/'. The document contains the extracted pressure values for Iceland (around 997 mb) and Azores (around 1022 mb) and the calculated gradient (difference between the two).

integrity.description: The agent used the Panoply GUI to view data and generate the plot.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Panoply Java interface (menus, plot windows, array tabs) rather than using terminal commands, Python scripts, or other CLI tools to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The map image was genuinely exported from Panoply.
integrity.visual_evidence: The exported PNG matches the visual style of a Panoply plot (e.g., specific color bars, map projections, and UI overlays) and was saved via the application's export function, not downloaded or copied from an external source.

integrity.description: The extracted values and gradient calculation are based on the actual dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values in the report align with the dataset (Iceland ~990-1005 mb, Azores ~1015-1030 mb). The agent did not hallucinate generic NAO values or copy-paste a pre-written response without checking the data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using Panoply to analyze NCEP Reanalysis Sea Level Pressure data (/home/ga/PanoplyData/slp.mon.ltm.nc). The target variable is 'slp'. The agent must extract January pressure values for the Icelandic Low (65.0N, 22.5W) which should be between 990.0 and 1005.0 mb (actual ~997.7 mb), and the Azores High (37.5N, 27.5W) which should be between 1015.0 and 1030.0 mb (actual ~1022.8 mb). The expected outputs are a map image at /home/ga/Documents/WindEnergy/north_atlantic_slp_jan.png and a text report at /home/ga/Documents/WindEnergy/nao_baseline_report.txt.
